title: Explore the .claude directory
description: Where Claude Code reads CLAUDE.md, settings.json, hooks, skills, commands, subagents, rules, and auto memory. Explore the .claude directory in your project and ~/.claude in your home directory.

text:
> Where Claude Code reads CLAUDE.md, settings.json, hooks, skills, commands, subagents, rules, and auto memory. Explore the .claude directory in your project and ~/.claude in your home directory.

Claude Code reads instructions, settings, skills, subagents, and memory from your project directory and from `~/.claude` in your home directory. Commit project files to git to share them with your team; files in `~/.claude` are personal configuration that applies across all your projects.

On Windows, `~/.claude` resolves to `%USERPROFILE%\.claude`. If you set `CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR`, every `~/.claude` path on this page lives under that directory instead.

Most users only edit `CLAUDE.md` and `settings.json`. The rest of the directory is optional: add skills, rules, or subagents as you need them.

breadcrumb: Explore the .claude directory > Explore the directory
text: Click files in the tree to see what each one does, when it loads, and an example.

cells: Enterprise-enforced settings that you can't override. See server-managed settings.
Your private preferences for this project, loaded alongside CLAUDE.md. Create it manually and add it to `.gitignore`.
Cloned marketplaces, installed plugin versions, and per-plugin data, managed by `claude plugin` commands. Orphaned versions are deleted 7 days after a plugin update or uninstall. See plugin caching.

breadcrumb: Explore the .claude directory > What's not shown
text:
The explorer covers files you author and edit. A few related files live elsewhere:

`~/.claude` also holds data Claude Code writes as you work: transcripts, prompt history, file snapshots, caches, and logs. See application data below.

cells: Give Claude project context and conventions
Allow or block specific tool calls
Run a script before or after tool calls
Set environment variables for the session
Keep personal overrides out of git
Add a prompt or capability you invoke with `/name`
Define a specialized subagent with its own tools

breadcrumb: Explore the .claude directory > Choose the right file
text: Different kinds of customization live in different files. Use this table to find where a change belongs.

Several things can override what you put in these files:

- Managed settings deployed by your organization take precedence over everything
- CLI flags like `--permission-mode` or `--settings` override `settings.json` for that session
- Some environment variables take precedence over their equivalent setting, but this varies: check the environment variables reference for each one

See settings precedence for the full order.

cells: Permissions, hooks, env vars, model defaults
Gitignored files to copy into new worktrees
Reusable prompts invoked with `/name` or auto-invoked
Single-file prompts; same mechanism as skills
Subagent definitions with their own prompt and tools
App state, OAuth, UI toggles, personal MCP servers
Auto memory: Claude's notes to itself across sessions

text:
This table lists every file the explorer covers. Project-scope files live in your repo under `.claude/` (or at the root for `CLAUDE.md`, `.mcp.json`, and `.worktreeinclude`). Global-scope files live in `~/.claude/` and apply across all projects.

Click a filename to open that node in the explorer above.
If a setting, hook, or file isn't taking effect, see Debug your configuration for the inspection commands and a symptom-first lookup table.

cells: Full conversation transcript: every message, tool call, and tool result
Large tool outputs spilled to separate files
Pre-edit snapshots of files Claude changed, used for checkpoint restore
Plan files written during plan mode
Per-session debug logs, written only when you start with `--debug` or run `/debug`
Contents of large pastes and attached images
Per-session task lists written by the task tools
Captured shell environment used by the Bash tool. Removed on clean exit. The sweep clears any left after a crash.
Timestamped copies of `~/.claude.json` taken before config migrations

breadcrumb: Explore the .claude directory > Application data > Cleaned up automatically
text: Files in the paths below are deleted on startup once they're older than `cleanupPeriodDays`. The default is 30 days.

cells: Every prompt you've typed, with timestamp and project path. Used for up-arrow recall.
Aggregated token and cost counts shown by `/usage`
Legacy per-session task lists. No longer written by current versions; safe to delete.

breadcrumb: Explore the .claude directory > Application data > Kept until you delete them
text:
The following paths are not covered by automatic cleanup and persist indefinitely.

Other small cache and lock files appear depending on which features you use and are safe to delete.

breadcrumb: Explore the .claude directory > Application data > Plaintext storage
text:
Transcripts and history are not encrypted at rest. OS file permissions are the only protection. If a tool reads a `.env` file or a command prints a credential, that value is written to `projects/<project>/<session>.jsonl`. To reduce exposure:

- Lower `cleanupPeriodDays` to shorten how long transcripts are kept
- Set the `CLAUDE_CODE_SKIP_PROMPT_HISTORY` environment variable to skip writing transcripts and prompt history in any mode. In non-interactive mode, you can instead pass `--no-session-persistence` alongside `-p`, or set `persistSession: false` in the Agent SDK.
- Use permission rules to deny reads of credential files

cells: Resume, continue, and rewind for past sessions
Nothing. Legacy directory not written by current versions.

breadcrumb: Explore the .claude directory > Application data > Clear local data
text:
You can delete any of the application-data paths above at any time. New sessions are unaffected. The table below shows what you lose for past sessions.

Don't delete `~/.claude.json`, `~/.claude/settings.json`, or `~/.claude/plugins/`: those hold your auth, preferences, and installed plugins.

Beyond the config you author, `~/.claude` holds data Claude Code writes during sessions. These files are plaintext. Anything that passes through a tool lands in a transcript on disk: file contents, command output, pasted text.

- Manage Claude's memory: write and organize CLAUDE.md, rules, and auto memory
- Configure settings: set permissions, hooks, environment variables, and model defaults
- Create skills: build reusable prompts and workflows
- Configure subagents: define specialized agents with their own context
